cards.back: A stack is a linear data structure that follows the LIFO (Last In First Out) principle

cards.front: What does a Stack offer in terms of Operations?
cards.back: A stack offers three primary operations: Push, Pop, and Peek

cards.front: How can a Stack be implemented using an Array?
cards.back: A Stack can be implemented using an Array by using two pointers, top and size, to keep track of the top element

cards.front: What is a common application of Stacks?
cards.back: A common application of Stacks is Evaluating Postfix Expressions

cards.front: What is the LIFO principle in the context of a Stack?
cards.back: The LIFO principle states that the last element added to the Stack is the first one to be removed
